steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The function is $K(v) = \frac{7}{2}v^2$, where $v$ is speed in meters per second and $K(v)$ is kinetic energy in joules. The notation $K(24) = 2{,}016$ means the input to the function is $v = 24$ and the output is $K = 2{,}016$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: The input $v = 24$ represents a speed of 24 meters per second, and the output $K = 2{,}016$ represents a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• Choice A: The object traveling at 24 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules. — This matches $v = 24$ and $K = 2{,}016$ exactly. That matches Choice A — The object traveling at 24 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules..
  • Choice B: The object traveling at 240 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules. — Eliminated.
  • Choice C: The object traveling at 2,016 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 24 joules. — Eliminated.
  • Choice D: The object traveling at 7,056 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 24 joules. — Eliminated.
